text.insert: The waterfall component owns one type of the
, the process-flow diagram block — the virtual form of a hand-drawn nested arrow flow. A block stores a typed recursive step tree in
; the arrow-tree notation is the text form of that tree —
writes it for the agent surface,
turns it back into steps on bulk import.
It is the vocabulary's third diagram type, and the three split by question:
How things relate — spatial boards of shapes, links, and embeds.
Exact exchanges — who sends what to whom, in what order.
How a process flows end to end — arrow steps, nested sublayers, and clarification notes on one rail.
Not a diagram type, but the adjacent call: reach for it when the nested structure is files, not steps.
A live block — the run-mode loop of the Melee decomp harness: one root, four arrow steps, nested sublayers, and two clarification notes that render as one bulleted card.

steps.text: Get epoch-size candidates from the ranked worker system
Exclude locked, cooled-down, or unschedulable work
Keep enough ready work to feed the worker pool
Spawn workers through the kernel until the epoch is drained
Each worker gets its own isolated worktree
the epoch boundary makes the map authoritative again
Move every item to its authoritative lane
Repeat until the operator stops or the run bound is reached

text.insert: The arrow-tree notation is the block's plain-text form:
writes it from the step tree and
parses it on bulk import, both in
. Six rules cover all of it:
A line with no arrow prefix starts a flow; the parser returns a forest, so one block can carry multiple roots.
An arrow line is one step of the flow.
A line's depth is the rank of its indent width among the distinct widths seen so far, sorted ascending.
Irregular indentation still nests, and a depth already assigned never changes when a new width appears later.
A backtick-wrapped span is a code value; the render gives it a code chip.
line is a clarification note on the step above it, not a step of its own.
lines group into one note card, rendered as bullets.
contract element — all state lives in typed props:

fields.name: steps
fields.description: Recursive step tree — the block's entire state.

fields.name: text
fields.description: Step text; backticks mark code values.

fields.name: kind
fields.description: "note" marks a clarification leaf; omitted reads as "step".

fields.name: steps
fields.description: Nested substeps; notes never carry them.

props.name: WaterfallState

text.insert: is the whole state: a recursive
Two omissions are deliberate: no title — a heading block above the waterfall carries one — and no stored text form — the arrow-tree notation exists only for import and the agent projection.
step that carries child steps — notes are leaves.
The block stores structure, not layout: no depths, no geometry enters the doc — depth derives from nesting at read time.
The type carries no delta text (
tolerantly — malformed entries are skipped, never thrown on.

operations.name: waterfall.setSteps
operations.description: Bulk replace: swap the entire step tree for the given steps — parse arrow-tree notation with parseWaterfall to build the tree from text.

params.name: steps
params.description: Complete replacement step tree; an empty array empties the waterfall.

operations.name: waterfall.insertStep
operations.description: Insert a step at an index path: the last element is the insert position among the addressed sibling list.

params.name: path
params.description: Index path; [i] inserts at position i among the roots, [a, ..., i] at position i under the step addressed by the prefix.

params.name: text
params.description: Step text; backticks mark code values.

params.name: kind
params.description: Step kind; "note" is a clarification leaf. Default "step".

operations.name: waterfall.setStepText
operations.description: Replace the text of the step at an index path.

params.name: path
params.description: Index path of the step, e.g. [0, 2] for the third child of the first root.

params.name: text
params.description: Replacement step text; backticks mark code values.

operations.name: waterfall.removeStep
operations.description: Remove the step at an index path, together with its entire subtree.

params.name: path
params.description: Index path of the step to remove, e.g. [0, 2].

operations.name: waterfall.moveStep
operations.description: Move the step at from (with its subtree) to the insert position to — to is interpreted against the tree after the step is removed.

params.name: from
params.description: Index path of the step to move.

params.name: to
params.description: Insertion index path (last element = insert position), resolved after the step is detached.

text.insert: Each action is one file in
Params validate against the action's TypeBox schema before
runs; each returns a shallow props patch.
is the bulk edit: it replaces the entire step tree in one validated call — an agent still edits by rewriting what it reads.
The four step actions address by index path: elements walk
is the third child of the first root — and insert paths end with the insert position.
after the moved step is detached, so a move within one sibling list uses post-removal indices.
Unresolvable paths, out-of-range insert positions, and inserts or moves under a note reject in the house pattern —
, wired through the descriptor in the same folder:
derives depth-computed nodes from the step tree; the viewer never parses, and the forest draws as monospace text.
The stylesheet injects once per document; the block scrolls in its own overflow container at a 640 px minimum width.
A block with no steps renders the placeholder line
Every flow is free text on one connected rail: a trunk drops from each parent, turns a rounded elbow into each child, and runs into an open-V arrowhead whose tip lands just before the text.

— indent sets the per-level inset and the elbow's left edge, arrow-gap is the distance from the arrowhead tip to the first letter, and the elbow lands on the first-line center while the trunk overlaps into both sibling gaps, so segments always meet.
The elbow's flat run is derived —
, clamped at zero — so it ends in the arrowhead's open back and the two stay connected at any knob values.
Every geometry var reads a style-rail token with the prototype default as fallback:
No kind colors, no icons, no collapse — the simplicity is deliberate, carried over from the pen-and-paper prototype.
Root lines render heaviest, depth-one steps semibold, and depth three and deeper dims to a 78% mix of the ink.
at the start of a segment and
anywhere in it render bold as keywords.
Backtick spans render as code chips on a muted background.
A note step renders as a bordered card — the component's only box — hung under its step, deliberately off the rail: no elbow, no arrowhead.
Consecutive note siblings collapse into one card, each note a bullet in its list.
Note text renders at the step ink and step text size by default —
to the text size — so the card keeps only its fill and border.
The block is a non-editable atom leaf (
); the shared atom node view reuses the same descriptor, so edit mode shows the same render as reading.
contract element: the markdown projection in
over the step tree. Projected, the live Example above is:

```waterfall
Run mode
     -> Get epoch-size candidates from the ranked worker system
          -> Exclude locked, cooled-down, or unschedulable work
          -> Keep enough ready work to feed the worker pool
     -> Drain the epoch with workers
          -> Spawn workers through the kernel until the epoch is drained
          -> Each worker gets its own isolated worktree
          > workers produce tentative evidence
          > the epoch boundary makes the map authoritative again
     -> Finish the epoch
          -> Run the full build
          -> Move every item to its authoritative lane
     -> Continue
          -> Repeat until the operator stops or the run bound is reached
```
— the block has no title; a heading above carries one.
The fence body is serialized arrow-tree notation — root lines bare,
Malformed props project an empty fence body rather than crashing.
contract element: the renderer paints from fifteen
tokens, defined in both theme blocks of
Fourteen knobs under Theme → Components → Waterfall: six color — Ink, Rail, Note text, Note background, Note border, Code background — plus eight length sliders.
Length sliders and ranges: Indent 16–72 px, Row gap 0–24 px, Arrow gap 0–16 px, Line height 16–40 px, Text size 10–18 px, Note text size 10–18 px, Arrow size 3–12 px, Stroke 0.5–4 px.
picker file in the Components section.
is a 78% color-mix of the ink — deliberately not a knob, so depth dimming tracks any ink change.
is opaque by contract: overlapping elbow and trunk strokes double alpha at the joins.

rows: Text ink — title and step lines
Step text at depth three and deeper
Horizontal inset per nesting level — also the elbow's left edge
Arrowhead tip to the first letter
Step first-line height — the elbow centers on half of it
Rail stroke width — trunks, elbows, arrowheads

text.insert: The type uses the default adapter — no agent of its own; the contract is
ops in the seven-op doc vocabulary (
), resolves the action, validates params, and runs
The returned props patch executes through the existing
path — merge semantics are single-sourced, the block id is preserved, and the inverse is the usual
Structural edits ride the generic ops —
never apply: the type carries no text.
